document.title: Post-Market Surveillance Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council
MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8 Guidelines on a medical devices vigilance system (January 2013)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer PMS plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines PMS data collection, assessment, trend reporting, authority communication, traceability, PMCF reference, and PSUR update/reporting approach for technical documentation.
keyPoints: The PMS plan is based on Article 83 and Annex III Section 1.1 and is part of technical documentation for devices other than custom-made devices.

PMS information to be reported in the PSUR includes sales volume, feedback and complaints, literature review, regulatory database and registry data, similar device information, and product quality information.
Customer surveys to healthcare professionals and distributors are conducted once in two years.

If analysed likelihood and severity are higher than previously estimated values, risk management and CAPA are implemented and the trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED and the notified body is informed.
The product is Class IIa and the PSUR will be updated at least every two years and reported when the certification authority requires it.

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation Document No. A-PMS-5-001, Rev. No. 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-PMS-5-001 Rev. No. 1

evidence.claim: The document date is October 20, 2023.
evidence.evidenceText: Post-market Surveillance Plan Rev. Date October 20, 2023

evidence.claim: The document covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The PMS plan references Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8.
evidence.evidenceText: Related References • Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council • MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8 Guidelines on a medical devices vigilance system (January 2013)

evidence.claim: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: PMS data collection includes sales volume, feedback and complaints, literature review, regulatory databases, similar device information, and product quality information.
evidence.evidenceText: The below information will be reported in PSUR refer to Section 15 of this document.

evidence.claim: Sales volume, feedback and complaints, literature review, public database data, and similar device information are collected once in two years.
evidence.evidenceText: The cycle of collecting data Once in two years

evidence.claim: Product quality information is collected monthly.
evidence.evidenceText: 5.6 Product quality information 2) The cycle of collecting data Monthly

evidence.claim: PMS data are analysed in the PSUR for new risks related to performance and safety of the device or similar devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The data from PMS are analysed whether there are new risks related to the performance and safety of our devices or similar devices in PSUR

evidence.claim: Risk assessment divides severity and probability into five stages.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk assessment divides severity and probability into 5 stages, records them in the risk management report, and manages them according to risk management procedures.

evidence.claim: If residual risk probability increases to more than level 3, the benefit-risk ratio should be reconsidered and risk management implemented.
evidence.evidenceText: If there is a result on the residual risk that could increase the occurrence of probability to more than level 3, the Benefit/risk ratio should reconsider, and risk management shall be implemented.

evidence.claim: Trend report monitoring collects relevant information over a six-month observation period.
evidence.evidenceText: Monitoring collects relevant information over 6 months observation period.

evidence.claim: If analysed likelihood and severity exceed previous estimates, risk management and CAPA are implemented and the trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED and the notified body is informed.
evidence.evidenceText: If the analysed likelihood and severity are higher than the previously estimated values, risk management and CAPA are implemented and trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED and inform NB.

evidence.claim: Information reported or managed through EUDAMED includes serious accidents and FSCA, periodic summary reports, trend reports, PSUR for Class III and Class IIb implantable products, and FSN.
evidence.evidenceText: The following information is reported/managed through the electronic system (EUDAMED). (1) Report by manufacturer on serious accidents and FSCA (2) Periodic summary reports by the manufacturer (3) Manufacturer’s summary report on trend (Trend Report) (4) PSUR (Class III and Class IIb implantable products) (5) FSN by the manufacturer

evidence.claim: The manufacturer should report to competent authorities and notify the EU representative and notified body.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer should report to the CA and notify to EU representative and NB.

evidence.claim: The EU representative is JaviTech e.K in Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany.
evidence.evidenceText: EU representative JaviTech e.K Sachsenhausener Straße 16, 65824 Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany

evidence.claim: The notified body is 3EC International a.s., notified body number 2265.
evidence.evidenceText: Notified Body 3EC International a.s. Notified Body number: 2265

evidence.claim: Traceability uses lot numbers, product name, manufacturing date, reference number, quantity, shipment place, and later UDI.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing date, Lot no., Ref. no., quantity and place of shipment are recorded to ensure traceability.

evidence.claim: The product is Class IIa and the PSUR is updated at least every two years and reported when the certification authority requires it.
evidence.evidenceText: Our product is Class IIa, so we will update the PSUR at least every two years and report the PSUR when the certification authority requires it.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority context is based on manufacturer technical documentation referencing Regulation (EU) 2017/745, EUDAMED, competent authorities, EU representative, and notified body; the source is not itself an authority-issued document.

Minor OCR or source text quality issues are present, including wording such as 'Public Databased', 'serious accidents', and 'Controlling and shopping products', but core metadata and regulatory facts remain readable.
